mission: Build the worlds most powerful AI-native terminal multiplexer — a single Rust binary that orchestrates teams of Claude agents across tmux panes with real-time dashboards, smart task routing, and zero-config GitHub integration

goals.title: Live tmux streaming dashboard
goals.description: Real-time web dashboard showing all agent panes with JSONL session events, terminal output, and smart project detection

goals.title: 206-tool MCP server
goals.description: Monolithic MCP server exposing all DX Terminal capabilities as tools for Claude agents

goals.title: Vision framework with GitHub sync
goals.description: Living product vision per project with change tracking, dashboard widget, and GitHub issue/milestone sync

goals.title: Autonomous agent orchestration
goals.description: Spawn, monitor, and auto-cycle Claude agents across panes with health checks and dead agent reaping

goals.title: Bidirectional build verification
goals.description: Two-pass BE↔FE test framework ensuring zero delta between backend data and frontend rendering

goals.title: GitHub Stardom — open source launch
goals.description: Prepare DX Terminal for public GitHub release with documentation, CI/CD, and community features

goals.title: Enterprise Documentation & QA
goals.description: Confluence-style wiki auto-generated from VDD vision files, plus integrated multi-framework test pipeline

milestones.title: Core Platform
milestones.description: Single Rust binary with MCP server, TUI, web dashboard, and tmux integration

milestones.title: Intelligence Layer
milestones.description: Vision framework, build verification, smart project detection, JSONL analytics

milestones.title: Enterprise QA & Documentation
milestones.description: Confluence-style wiki, 8-framework QA engine, VDD pipeline automation

milestones.title: Public Launch
milestones.description: Open source release on GitHub with docs, CI/CD, and community engagement

architecture.title: Single Rust binary
architecture.decision: Ship everything as one binary (dx) — MCP server, web server, TUI, all tools
architecture.rationale: Simplest deployment, no dependency management, instant startup

architecture.title: include_str HTML embedding
architecture.decision: Embed dashboard HTML at compile time via include_str! — no separate static files
architecture.rationale: Single binary deployment, no file path issues, version-locked UI

architecture.title: WebSocket for real-time data
architecture.decision: Use WebSocket for all live dashboard data instead of SSE or polling
architecture.rationale: Bidirectional (commands + data), single connection, lower latency than SSE

architecture.title: JSONL session scanning for project detection
architecture.decision: Scan Claude JSONL tool_use entries for most-referenced project path when cwd is generic
architecture.rationale: Tmux pane cwd is often ~/Projects (parent), but tool calls reveal the actual project being worked on

changes.reason: First vision creation for DX Terminal

changes.new_value: answered: In vision.json with optional sub-vision files
changes.reason: Keeps it simple. Sub-visions only when a feature is complex enough.

features.title: Vision-Driven Development
features.description: Recursive vision tree with features, questions, decisions, tasks

questions.text: Should features be stored in vision.json or separate files?
questions.answer: In vision.json with optional sub-vision files

decisions.decision: In vision.json with optional sub-vision files
decisions.rationale: Keeps it simple. Sub-visions only when a feature is complex enough.

features.title: Confluence-Style Wiki Page
features.description: Auto-generated wiki at /wiki?project=NAME rendering full vision.json: principles, milestones, goals, features with tasks/criteria/questions/decisions, ADRs, changelog, research docs

questions.text: Should wiki be embedded HTML or separate template engine?
questions.answer: Embedded HTML generated by Rust handler — consistent with ADR2 (include_str pattern)

decisions.decision: Embedded HTML generated by Rust handler
decisions.rationale: Consistent with ADR2. No template engine dependency. Full control over layout.

tasks.title: Build wiki_page handler in api.rs
tasks.description: Axum handler that reads vision.json and generates full HTML wiki page

tasks.title: Add /wiki route to Axum router
tasks.description: Register wiki_page at /wiki in mod.rs build_router

tasks.title: Add Wiki link to dashboard
tasks.description: Add Wiki ↗ link in vision cockpit header with dynamic project param

tasks.title: Render all vision sections
tasks.description: Principles, milestones with progress bars, goals with metrics, features with tasks/criteria/questions/decisions, ADRs, changelog

acceptance_criteria: All vision.json sections displayed: principles, milestones, goals, features, ADRs, changelog
Features show tasks with status badges, acceptance criteria, questions, decisions
Wiki link visible in dashboard vision cockpit
No JavaScript errors on page load

features.title: 8-Framework QA Engine
features.description: Enterprise testing pipeline: unit, integration, E2E smoke, E2E flows, visual regression, chaos engineering, security, accessibility — auto-triggered by VDD hooks

decisions.decision: QA engine as Claude Code skills, not compiled code
decisions.rationale: Skills can leverage Playwright MCP, curl, and all tools. No compilation needed.

tasks.title: Rewrite /qa skill with 8 frameworks
tasks.description: Complete QA engine with unit, integration, E2E, visual, chaos, security, a11y, performance

tasks.title: Create VDD PostToolUse hook
tasks.description: vdd-test-trigger.py watches vision.json edits, auto-triggers /qa when feature enters test phase

tasks.title: Upgrade Stop hook Engine 3
tasks.description: Differentiate test-phase vs build-phase features, enforce QA completion

tasks.title: Create supporting test skills
tasks.description: test-run, test-gate, test-visual, test-write, test-fix skills

acceptance_criteria: PostToolUse hook triggers QA on feature test-phase entry
Stop hook enforces QA completion before agent can stop
DORA metrics tracked per QA run

features.title: Open Source Documentation
features.description: Write README and docs for GitHub launch
